title.text: How to archive a Go Vocal platform

body:0001.text: Why you may need to archive your platform

body:0002.text: For compliance or legal reasons, you may need to archive your Go Vocal platform and store a copy of its content. This guide explains how to configure your archiving tool to work correctly with Go Vocal.

body:0004.text: Go Vocal's frontend is a single-page application (SPA) built with JavaScript. Most web archiving tools use a scraper that visits every page and saves the HTML. However, many of these scrapers don't execute JavaScript — or don't execute it fully enough — which results in blank pages being saved.

body:0005.text: If this is the case with your scraping software, to solve this, Go Vocal provides a prerendering service that can serve fully rendered, static HTML to tools that identify themselves as crawlers. When the platform detects that a request comes from an archiving tool, it:

body:0006.text: Serves a pure HTML version of the page instead of the JavaScript-based SPA.

body:0007.text: Bypasses the cookie consent manager, so the actual page content is visible without user interaction.

body:0008.text: How to configure your archiving tool

body:0010.text: You can trigger the prerendered HTML version of the platform in one of two ways.

body:0012.text: Configure your archiving tool to append _escaped_fragment_ as a query parameter to all your requests. For example:

body:0013.text: When the platform detects this parameter, it will serve a fully rendered HTML version of the page.

body:0014.text: Option 2: Use a recognized User-Agent string

body:0015.text: Make sure your archiving tool sends a User-Agent header that is recognized by Go Vocal. The following archiving tools are currently supported:

body:0019.text: Heritrix (also used by Archive-It and many national libraries)

body:0021.text: Internet Archive / Wayback Machine

body:0026.text: Bibliothèque nationale de France web archive

body:0030.text: 💡 Tip: If your archiving software allows you to set a custom User-Agent string, setting it to include the name of one of the supported tools above is the easiest way to get it working.

body:0031.text: What if my tool isn't recognized?

body:0032.text: If your archiving tool uses a User-Agent string that is not in the list above and you cannot change it, use Option 1 instead — appending _escaped_fragment_ to your request URLs.

body:0033.text: If neither option works for your setup, please contact support and we can look into adding your tool's User-Agent to our recognized list.

body:0034.text: B. Covering all public facing content using /sitemap.xml

body:0035.text: The platform contains some dynamic content, for example the “Load more” buttons in longer lists of projects or inputs. If your web archiving tool is configured to only act like a spider - this means it explores the platform merely by following internal links - the content behind these dynamic elements will not be discovered, as the buttons are not functional in the prerendered pages.

body:0036.text: To solve this, make sure your archiving tool makes use of the /sitemap.xml file served from your platform. It lists all pages, to make the archive complete. Most scraping software will do this out of the box, but it’s worthwhile to check.
